summary: Validated new image upload + multi-image carousel feature end-to-end. Backend: 20/20 pytest pass (added new test_upload_and_carousel.py covering POST /api/upload, GET /api/files/{path}, project create/update with images[]). Frontend (Playwright): admin dropzone uploads 3 PNGs, thumbnails render with Cover badge + remove buttons, project persists with images[], appears in admin + public portfolio with 'Views' badge, detail modal opens ImageCarousel with prev/next/counter/thumbnail navigation working (verified visually 2/3 counter + active thumb in screenshot). Seeded single-image projects gracefully fallback (no prev/next shown). Inquiry form + footer regression pass.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py init_storage() is sync and called during startup; if Emergent storage is briefly unavailable on boot, uploads still re-init lazily, but startup logs an error - acceptable.
handleImageUpload uploads sequentially in a for-loop; for 7 images this can be slow. Could be parallelised with Promise.all (minor perf optimisation).
ImageCarousel resets idx based on `title` changing (useEffect deps). If two projects share the same title, idx won't reset. Use the project id instead for safety.
App.js is now ~1452 lines. Splitting ImageCarousel, AdminPanel, ProjectDetailModal into their own files would improve maintainability.

removeProjectImage updates image_url to images[0] but doesn't repopulate image_url if user removes the cover and uploads more later (works because handleImageUpload sets image_url only if empty). Minor edge case OK.
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 4 projects + 1 settings doc. Test created/cleaned: TEST_UI Carousel Project (uploaded 3 images, deleted at end). Pytest TEST_ data cleaned by delete steps.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Object storage initialized via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY at startup. Upload endpoint returns {url:'/api/files/<path>', path}. Frontend stores those urls in projectForm.images and persists them; ImageCarousel falls back to [image_url] for legacy projects without images[]. test_upload_and_carousel.py uses Pillow to generate small PNGs.
